nodes.text: Hey. Rough day or easy — let's take a quick look at it.
How would you describe today in one word?

match: Productive
Mixed
Tough
Frustrating

nodes.text: What do you think drove today's positive moments?
options: The right people showed up for me

match: I planned it well
I adapted when things changed
The right people showed up for me
Circumstances just aligned

nodes.text: When something went wrong today, what caused it?
options: I saw it coming but couldn't stop it

match: I wasn't prepared enough
I saw it coming but couldn't stop it
Someone else made it harder
Circumstances weren't in my favour

nodes.text: When there was a moment to step in today — what did you do?
options: Yes, I made a call and it shaped the outcome
I tried but wasn't sure it was working
I noticed the moment but hesitated
Not really, the window felt closed

nodes.text: Looking back — did you have more influence today than you felt?
options: Yes, I had a choice I didn't take
Maybe, not sure what I'd have done
I saw it but the situation left no room
No, it was genuinely out of my hands

match: Yes, I made a call and it shaped the outcome
I tried but wasn't sure it was working
I noticed the moment but hesitated
Not really, the window felt closed
Yes, I had a choice I didn't take
Maybe, not sure what I'd have done
I saw it but the situation left no room
No, it was genuinely out of my hands

nodes.text: Looking at how you handled today — what does it tell you?
options: I showed up the way I wanted to
I could have been more deliberate
I fell back on old habits

nodes.text: If you could replay one moment today — what would you change?
options: I'd have asked for help earlier
I'd have taken a breath before reacting

nodes.text: On days when you feel most like yourself — what's different?
options: I feel like my effort matters
People around me are more supportive
I'm less in my own head
Those days feel far away right now

nodes.text: You stayed in the driver's seat today. Not because everything went right — but because you didn't hand the wheel to circumstances. That's the thing.
You can see the moment now — where you had a choice. Agency doesn't always show up in real time. The goal is just to shorten that gap.
Tough days make the walls feel closer. But somewhere in today you made a call — even a small one. That counts.
Okay. Now let's shift — from how you handled things, to what you gave.
Think of one interaction today. What was your role in it?
options: I was doing my own thing

match: I was helping someone
I was doing my own thing
I was getting help
I was waiting for something

nodes.text: Did you do something today that wasn't expected of you?
options: Yes, helped someone without being asked
Yes, flagged something outside my lane
Sort of, stayed longer than needed
No, I stuck to my work

nodes.text: Did your effort feel seen today?
options: No, I worked hard and it went unnoticed
No, someone else got credit for my work
Somewhat, but I let it go

match: Yes, helped someone without being asked
Yes, flagged something outside my lane
Sort of, stayed longer than needed
No, I stuck to my work
Yes, I worked hard and no one saw it
Yes, someone else got credit for my work
A little, but I let it go
Not really, wasn't on my mind

nodes.text: When you helped today — what was driving it?
options: It felt like the right thing
I'd want the same if I were them
Wanted to look like a team player

nodes.text: When you felt overlooked — what did you do with it?
options: Kept going, recognition isn't the point
Mentioned it to someone I trust
It stayed with me all day

nodes.text: If a colleague needed you today — outside your job — what would you have done?
options: Helped, but I'd notice if it kept happening
Pointed them to the right person

match: It felt like the right thing
Easier than watching them struggle
I'd want the same if I were them
Wanted to look like a team player
Kept going, recognition isn't the point
Mentioned it to someone I trust
Pulled back a little
It stayed with me all day
Helped, no question
Helped, but I'd notice if it kept happening
Depended on what it was
Pointed them to the right person

nodes.text: Did your presence shift anything for someone today?
options: I think so, hard to know

nodes.text: On a day you go home feeling good — what made it that way?
What did you find yourself wanting more of today?
options: A team that pulls their weight

nodes.text: You gave today — not because someone was watching. That kind of effort is quiet and irreplaceable. Teams run on it.
A mix today — some giving, some expecting. That's honest. The question is whether recognition drives what you give, or follows from it.
Something feels off balance. Worth sitting with — not to judge, just to ask whether what you want from work is connected to what you're putting in.
Last one — from what you gave, to who you were thinking about.
When you think about today's biggest challenge — who comes to mind?
options: A specific person who had it harder

match: Just me
My team
A specific person who had it harder
The people we serve

nodes.text: While dealing with your own stuff — did you notice anyone else?
When you thought about someone else today — did it change what you did?
options: It added pressure but also meaning
Not really, I still focused on my part

match: No, I was too focused
I was too stretched
I noticed but didn't engage
Yes, I checked in anyway
Yes, I made different choices
It made me push harder
It added pressure but also meaning
Not really, I still focused on my part

nodes.text: If a colleague had a rough day today — do you know?
options: I think so, but said nothing
We don't really talk about that

nodes.text: Did you stop to see today from someone else's side?
options: Yes, I tried to understand their view
I was focused on outcomes not feelings

nodes.text: Who felt most real to you today?

match: No idea, I didn't notice
We don't really talk about that
I think so, but said nothing
Yes, I reached out
Yes, I tried to understand their view
Briefly
I was focused on outcomes not feelings
No, didn't have the bandwidth
One specific person
The team as a whole
The end user we serve
Honestly, it was abstract

nodes.text: When you felt most alive at work — was someone else part of it?
options: Yes, it was about helping someone
Sort of, but the work mattered more
No, just me and the work

nodes.text: If you'd looked up from your own day — what would you have seen?
options: I don't know, I wasn't looking

nodes.text: When you helped someone today — what did it do for you?
options: Reminded me why I do this
Gave me more energy than my own work
It drained me more than it filled me

nodes.text: Today was mostly about you — your challenge, your effort. That's okay. But the kind of meaning that sticks usually involves someone else. Tomorrow, notice one person before yourself.
You're starting to look up. Not fully there yet — but noticing means the shift is already happening.
You thought beyond yourself today. That's harder than it sounds on a difficult day. People feel it — even when they don't say it.

axis1.internal: You owned today — the wins and the friction.
axis1.external: Today felt out of your hands. Noticing that is its own kind of awareness.
axis1.mixed: You moved between ownership and overwhelm. Both are honest.

axis2.contribution: You gave more than you took today.
axis2.entitlement: The scales felt off. Worth sitting with — no judgment.
axis2.mixed: Partly giving, partly expecting. That's most days honestly.

axis3.other: Your circle of concern was wide today. That's rare.
axis3.self: Today was a solo journey. Try widening the lens tomorrow.
axis3.expanding: You're building the habit of thinking beyond yourself.

closingInsights: Small honest answers, night after night, build something larger than any single day.
The point isn't a perfect day. It's seeing the one you actually had.
Growth isn't dramatic. It's this — showing up to look at yourself clearly.

nodes.text: That's it. Get some rest. See you tomorrow.
